topic: Two strangers realize they've been dating the same person
raw_output:
[story 1]

The coffee shop hummed with the usual midday bustle. I was waiting for my latte, my gaze drifting across the room until it snagged on him. He was laughing, that specific, slightly crooked way he tilted his head when someone said something witty. A jolt went through me, a sudden, sharp awareness that made my hand freeze halfway to my cup. Then, across the crowded expanse, I saw her. She was wearing the exact same faded blue sweater I’d seen him wearing yesterday, and she was leaning in to whisper something to him, her smile mirroring his perfectly. My breath hitched. I watched them interact, a strange knot tightening in my chest. I took a large gulp of lukewarm coffee, my focus entirely fractured, trying to reconcile the two identical figures with the man I’d just been observing.

[story 2]

It was the park bench, bathed in the amber glow of late afternoon sun. I was trying to read, but my eyes kept flicking up. There he was, sketching in a worn notebook, his brow furrowed in concentration. Then, a figure approached, and the way he turned his body, the slight adjustment of his jacket—it was uncanny. He looked up, and his eyes met mine. A flicker of recognition, something deeply familiar, crossed his face before he offered a small, knowing smile. My fingers tightened around the spine of my book. I lowered it slowly, my knuckles white. I watched as he spoke, his voice a low, melodic murmur, and then I saw her arrive, carrying a book identical to mine, and she settled beside him with an ease that felt entirely too practiced. I stood up abruptly, needing distance, my steps clumsy on the gravel path.

[story 3]

The gallery opening was overwhelming, a sea of polite chatter and expensive perfume. I found myself standing near a sculpture, watching a man across the room. He was animated, gesturing with his hands as he explained something, and I found myself leaning against a pillar, trying to absorb his energy. Suddenly, I saw her approach, drawn by the same magnetic pull. She paused beside him, and for a split second, their eyes locked—a shared history shimmering between them. A strange hollowness opened in my stomach. I watched them converse, the easy rhythm of their exchange seeming to bypass me entirely. I pulled out my phone, intending to check a message, but my thumb hovered over the screen. I just stood there, feeling disconnected, as they turned toward the exit together, a perfect unit.
